narrative.summary: Chef Chatchalit Chuayruk transformed the original Pii Nong into something dramatically more ambitious — ancient Thai recipes revived with modern technique. Dishes you've never seen on a Thai menu because they haven't been on one in decades. 4.5★ (1020 reviews).

blocks.title: The Space
blocks.body: You're on a stretch of Bayview where the neighbourhood shifts from residential to low-rise commercial, the kind of block where you'd expect a dentist's office, not what's waiting inside. The dining room feels refreshed but unfussy—clean lines, warm wood tones, tables spaced wide enough for conversation. Staff move through the space with the kind of attentiveness that suggests they actually want you to enjoy yourself.

blocks.title: The Food
blocks.body: Chef Chatchalit Chuayruk is cooking dishes that disappeared from Thai menus generations ago—ancient recipes he's revived with technique that makes them feel urgent and new. This isn't the pad thai you know: expect curry-spiked versions, butterfly coconut shrimp with a brittle golden shell, and cashew chicken that arrives glistening and deeply savoury (order rice separately, it doesn't come with). The menu reads like an excavation project, each dish a small act of preservation made vivid on the plate.

blocks.title: Go When
blocks.body: Bring someone who thinks they know Thai food and wants to be surprised. Or come on a weeknight when you need capable service and flavours sharp enough to shake off the day.

blocks.title: The Vibe
blocks.body:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "We had such a great experience at Pii Nong! The food was absolutely delicious and full of flavor. The butterfly coconut shrimp was perfectly crispy, the vegetable spring rolls were light and tasty, and the cashew chicken was cooked beautifully (just a tip: it doesn’t come with rice, so order some on the side). The curry pad thai was outstanding, rich, aromatic, and so satisfying.

On top of the amazing food, the service was excellent, friendly, attentive, and made us feel really welcome. It’s rare to find a spot that nails both food and service, but Pii Nong definitely does. Highly recommend if you’re craving authentic Thai in Toronto!"

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "We stopped in while we were in the neighbourhood and are so glad we did. The food here is excellent and staff are lovely. We had the shrimp fresh rolls and butterfly coconut shrimp to start, and the cashew chicken and curry pad Thai as mains. The coconut shrimp were perfectly fried and the sauce with each app was delicious. Portions for mains are very generous and the food has a good spice level. FYI the cashew chicken did not come with rice so we added an order. We had the mango sticky rice and coconut ice cream for dessert. Both were lovely and a nice way to end the meal. I would eat here again for sure"

⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Pii Nong is a cute classy vibe. I’m not the biggest fan of the location but it’s a place you should definitely try. Beware it’s a very high traffic area, paid parking is available near by, there’s a few lots or street parking.

Food came out fairly quick. We got the chicken green curry and I was it was spicier but it was more mild & coconut base. The crispy beef was one of my favourites, super flavourful! A must try! Pad See Ew was good, I’ve had better but still tasted good, we asked for extra spicy and it was spicy.  The fried rice has bigger chunks of pinapple, it wasn’t as sweet as I would have liked it to be but it was good. I love the large shrimp it came with!

I would recommend trying this place at-least once.  I was too full to order a drink but a lot of people were drinking the Teddy Bear Tea, it was super cute to see !!"
